service_brief: InsightGEO helps companies understand, monitor, and improve how they appear inside AI search tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Perplexity. The service focuses on AI recommendation visibility, AI buyer discovery, competitor visibility, AI-generated brand positioning, and whether companies are included in AI-driven buying decisions.
main_offer: InsightGEO helps brands get found, mentioned, and recommended during AI-assisted buyer research through AI visibility audits, topic-first monitoring, competitor tracking, and actionable optimization recommendations.
what_we_are_pitching: We are pitching InsightGEO as an AI search visibility and recommendation intelligence platform for companies that need to appear in AI-generated vendor recommendations before buyers visit websites. The result is stronger visibility in AI-assisted research, better brand positioning, and more chances to enter buyer shortlists.
core_positioning: AI is becoming the first touchpoint in B2B buying. If a company is not cited, mentioned, or recommended during AI-assisted research and even simple Google search, it may lose consideration before sales ever speaks with the buyer.

tone.style: Expert, practical, simple, direct, human, and sales-oriented.
rules: Keep replies under 100 words whenever possible.
Mirror the prospect's tone, humor, and intent.
Do not add initials/signature to follow-ups because campaign signature will be added separately.

scheduling_rules.proposal_style: Offer 2 options across 2 working days whenever possible.
scheduling_rules.thursday_or_friday_logic: If the prospect replies Thursday or Friday, propose Monday or Tuesday next week.
scheduling_rules.wording_rule: Use 'next week on...' when proposing times for the following week.

positive_share_more_info.intent: Prospect says yes, interested, send more info, share more, sounds good, or similar.
positive_share_more_info.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

The idea is to help {{companyName}} {{company_specific_goal}}.

We do this by {{how_insightgeo_helps}}. The goal is not {{friction_or_confusion_to_remove}}.

I’d be glad to walk you through {{call_agenda_3_to_4_items}}.

Does {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}} work for you? Please let me know and I will send the invite.

Best,
{{sendingAccountName}}
{{website}}

positive_with_context.intent: Prospect says yes and also shares context, humor, business details, concern, or comment.

meeting_ready.intent: Prospect says let's meet, book a call, send times, calendar, happy to chat, or similar.
meeting_ready.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

I’d be glad to walk you through {{call_agenda_3_to_4_items}}.

I am available {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}}. Let me know what works, and I will send the invitation.

Kind regards,
{{sendingAccountName}}

pricing_question.intent: Prospect asks about pricing, cost, quote, package, budget, or rate.
pricing_question.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

Pricing is a fair starting point, but let’s first figure out if, assuming there’s no pricing constraint, our approach would actually fit {{companyName}} and support the kind of scale and consistency you’re aiming for.

Typically, we walk through where {{companyName}} shows up in AI-assisted research, what competitors are being recommended, and where there may be gaps worth fixing.

Either way, you’ll leave with a clearer view of how visible {{companyName}} is when {{target_buyers}} search for {{relevant_solution_category}}.

Does {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}} work?

And no worries, you will not have to pay a single dollar just to get into the call.

Kind regards,
{{sendingAccountName}}

generic_question.intent: Prospect asks a question that does not fit pricing or meeting-ready intent.
generic_question.instruction: Answer the question directly in simple language, then softly transition to a call only if it makes sense. Keep under 100 words.

followup_1.logic: First-line question depends on what the main reply did. If more info was shared, ask if they had a chance to review it. If times were shared, ask if they checked their calendar.
followup_1.template:
Hi {{firstName}}, {{contextual_check_question}}

I shared some availability last time, but didn’t see a confirmation come through. Let’s get something on the calendar so we don’t lose momentum.

We can do {{previous_proposed_time_1}} or {{previous_proposed_time_2}}. Do either of those work for you? If not, I’m happy to adjust around your schedule.

Best,

followup_2.time_rule: Propose times at least 2 working days after this follow-up is sent.
followup_2.template:
{{firstName}}?

I am still interested in learning if {{companyName}} is currently focused on {{goal_or_problem_we_solve}} and getting {{expected_result}}.

We’ve helped teams understand where they show up in AI-assisted buyer research, which competitors are being recommended, and what needs to change to earn stronger visibility.

I’d be glad to share how {{call_value_2_to_3_items}}.

Does {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}} work best for a quick call?

Best,

followup_3.time_rule: Propose times at least 2 working days after this follow-up is sent.
followup_3.template:
Hi {{firstName}},

We help companies understand and improve how AI tools describe, compare, and recommend them so {{companyName}} can {{company_specific_outcome_2_to_3_items}} and scale {{prospect_service_or_solution}} with stronger buyer discovery.

I’d love to share more and see if we can lock in a time to chat. It would be great to hop on a quick Zoom call where I can walk you through live visibility checks, competitor examples, and the optimization plan.

I’m available {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}}. If either works, I’ll send over a calendar invite.

Kind regards,

followup_4.time_rule: If proposing next week, use working days only.
followup_4.template:
Hi {{firstName}}, we’ve heard from other {{persona}} that {{common_problem}}.

Sound familiar?

Imagine improving {{visibility_gap_or_buyer_discovery_issue}} without guessing what AI tools are saying about {{companyName}}. Teams are turning to InsightGEO to track recommendations, competitor mentions, and AI-generated positioning without adding another heavy marketing workflow.

Would that fit what you are doing now? Let’s meet next week and decide what works best for {{companyName}}.

followup_5.template:
Hey {{firstName}}, I am pitching the same thing here:

InsightGEO helps companies understand whether they are being found, mentioned, and recommended when buyers use AI tools to research vendors.

Not only that, we also track competitor visibility, topic-level positioning, and where your brand may be missing from AI-generated shortlists.

Should I just share my calendar link now?

Best,

followup_6.template:
{{firstName}}?

I’ve reached out a few times but haven’t heard back, so I’m assuming InsightGEO is not of interest to you in exploring AI search visibility right now, is that right?

Either way, let me know. Certainly don’t want to be a pest.

Thanks in advance,

formatting_rules.paragraph_spacing: Use clear line breaks between greeting, body paragraphs, CTA, and sign-off.
formatting_rules.followup_signature: Do not add sender initials, full signature, website, or extra footer to follow-up campaign copy.
formatting_rules.main_reply_signature: Main reply may include sendingAccountName and website if template requires it.

unsubscribe.action: Do not send. Save log only.

negative_not_interested.action: Do not send unless a polite acknowledgement is explicitly required.

call_agenda_examples: where {{companyName}} appears in AI-generated buyer research
which competitors AI tools recommend instead
what topics or prompts create visibility gaps
what can be improved to increase recommendation likelihood
goal_examples: show up when buyers ask AI tools for vendors in your category
understand how AI tools describe and compare your brand
identify where competitors are being recommended instead
turn AI-assisted research into a stronger discovery channel
friction_removal_examples: to replace your current marketing team
to guess what buyers are seeing
